title: Ensure that the --kubeconfig kubelet.conf file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the --kubeconfig kubelet.conf file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the kubelet.conf file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The kubelet.conf file is the Kubernetes configuration file for the node, and controls various parameters that set the behavior and identity of the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the following command to view the user and group ownership of the kubelet.conf file: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf done OR for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /host/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf done Verify that the ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.default_value: By default, kubelet.conf file ownership is set to root:root . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Open the IBM Cloud Console and navigate to the OpenShift cluster management section.
2. Access the terminal for a specific node by using the `oc` command line tools. Run the command: `oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'` to list the names of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster.
3. For each node retrieved, execute the following command: `oc debug node/<node_name> -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf` (or `chroot /host/stat -c %U:%G /host/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf` if needed). Replace `<node_name>` with the appropriate node's name.
4. Verify the output to see the ownership of the `kubelet.conf` file.
5. Ensure that the ownership is set to `root:root` for compliance.
6. If the ownership is not `root:root`, note the findings for remediation purposes.

Expected secure configuration:
- Ownership of `/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf` should be `root:root`.

How to confirm compliance:
- Successful verification will show ownership as `root:root` for the `kubelet.conf` file across all nodes in the OpenShift cluster. If it does, the control is compliant. If it does not, document the nodes that are non-compliant for further review.
